task: I’m replying to a client who keeps circling back to the same point, and I want to get the message out cleanly without spending too much time on it. Can you help me draft a first pass I can quickly adjust before I send it?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — send me:

1. The client’s message or the point they keep repeating  
2. Your preferred tone: firm, friendly, diplomatic, or very direct  
3. Any key facts or boundary you want included  
4. Whether you want it short and crisp or a bit more polished

If you want, I can also give you a quick fill-in template right away:

> Hi [Name],  
> Thanks for raising this again. I want to make sure I’m addressing it clearly: [main point].  
> As mentioned, [brief explanation / boundary / decision].  
> At this stage, the next step is [next step], and I’m happy to help with that.  
> Best,  
> [Your Name]

Paste the client’s note and I’ll turn it into a clean first draft.

user_simulator.text:
I don’t have the client’s note pasted, so here’s the actual point: they keep pushing back on the same thing — that my fee is tied to the scope, and any extra back-and-forth beyond the agreed review is changing the job. I want it firm, not rude. Short. Something like: 

Hi [Name],

I want to be clear on this point: the current fee covers the agreed scope only. Anything beyond that is additional work and would need to be billed separately.

If you want to move forward with the revised scope, I can send over the updated terms.

Best,
[Your Name]

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter first pass that stays firm without sounding harsh:

> Hi [Name],  
> Just to clarify, the current fee applies to the agreed scope only. Any additional revisions or back-and-forth beyond that would be considered extra work and billed separately.  
> If you’d like to proceed with the revised scope, I’m happy to send updated terms.  
> Best,  
> [Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly softer version, or
- a more assertive version that closes the loop more firmly.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, not mushy.
